commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a core `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list` CLI, a local SQLite snapshot provider and manifest repository, a shared `VACUUM INTO` helper, named global/agent targets, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +730, Tests +716, Docs +103. Total +1549 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR for a new operator workflow. Source review shows focused tests exercise the intended path, but packaged CLI behavior has not been proven.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is required because the remaining blockers are maintainer acceptance of a new core CLI/artifact contract, protected-label/draft sequencing, and contributor real behavior proof rather than a narrow automation-repairable defect.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body:
## Summary

This replaces the earlier PR1-4 stack with one coherent core snapshot MVP.

Adds:
- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository
- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`
- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`
- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files
- safe-sync workflow proof: create a snapshot, copy only the completed artifact directory, restore from that copy, and verify the restored SQLite database
- CLI docs that say which files are syncable and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored
- CLI docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup`: snapshot is a per-database SQLite sync artifact; backup is a broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive

## Why

The operational goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.

This PR establishes that boundary:

- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state
- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`
- hosts/containers/object-store sync jobs should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family
- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens it

This is intentionally different from `openclaw backup`: backup remains the broad local recovery archive for OpenClaw state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces. Snapshot is the narrow SQLite-safe artifact that backup systems or host sync loops can copy safely.

## Stack

This PR now combines the previous core implementation slices:

- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof
- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI
- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets
- this PR: safe-sync artifact and restore proof/docs

Follow-up validation PR: #94967
RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20
Related: #94675, #94646

## Scope

This does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, source test harness with temporary SQLite databases, WAL mode enabled, copied snapshot directories, named OpenClaw database targets, and local restore targets.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
2. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/docs.json docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/backup.md docs/cli/index.md`
3. `git diff --check`
4. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
5. `pnpm docs:check-links`
6. `pnpm docs:list`
7. Codex review: `codex review --base origin/giodl/snapshot-named-targets-pr3`, then rerun after the docs navigation fix

Evidence after fix:
Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests). `oxfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, `docs:check-mdx`, `docs:check-links`, and `docs:list` completed cleanly. Codex review initially found one P3 docs navigation gap; adding `cli/snapshot` to `docs/docs.json` cleared the rerun with no actionable findings. A follow-up docs-only check also passed for the snapshot-versus-backup clarification.

Observed result after fix:
The snapshot tests create WAL-mode SQLite databases, produce snapshot artifact directories, verify manifests and hashes, list snapshots, restore into fresh targets, remove stale restore-side sidecars, run SQLite integrity checks, and read expected restored rows. The named-target tests cover both `--target global` and `--agent <id>` while preserving the explicit `--db` path.

What was not tested:
No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.
